display.purpose: KPI values and primary numeric summaries.

title.purpose: Chart titles and compact panel headings.

body.purpose: General UI text and inherited control text.

label.purpose: KPI labels, strip labels, axis labels, and status support.

shadows.name: tooltip-drop
shadows.purpose: Separate chart tooltips from the plot area.

shadows.name: status-glow
shadows.purpose: Reinforce live, updating, stale, and offline status dots.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Panel hover, control hover, and status changes.

motion.name: chart-update
motion.purpose: Realtime updates should feel immediate rather than choreographed.

components.name: Segment Control
components.description: Compact local chart filter with inverted active state.

components.name: Select Control
components.description: Chart-local select for expiry or window controls.
components.css: .ds-select{height:1.75rem;display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:.35rem;color:var(--terminal-muted,#f2f2f28c);font-size:.75rem;line-height:1;position:relative;font-family:Outfit,sans-serif}.ds-select select{appearance:none;height:1.75rem;color:var(--color,#f2f2f2);background:var(--background,#0d0d0d);border:2px solid var(--border-color,#ffffff1a);border-radius:.375rem;outline:none;padding:0 1.55rem 0 .5rem;transition:border-color .2s}.ds-select select:hover,.ds-select select:focus{border-color:var(--color,#f2f2f2)}.ds-select:after{content:"";pointer-events:none;width:.45rem;height:.45rem;border-right:1.5px solid var(--color,#f2f2f2);border-bottom:1.5px solid var(--color,#f2f2f2);opacity:.65;position:absolute;right:.55rem;top:50%;transform:translateY(-65%) rotate(45deg)}

components.name: Chart Panel
components.description: Flat terminal container for a Highcharts SVG plot and chart-local controls.

components.name: KPI Strip Cell
components.description: Dense metric cell inside the continuous KPI strip.

components.name: Highcharts Tooltip
components.description: Dark SVG tooltip treatment used for chart hover values.

components.name: Top Bar Status
components.description: Persistent live/stale/reconnecting state and latest snapshot timestamp.

narrative.overview: The system is a black, high-density market surface built for traders who need to read volatility, positioning, and flow without being slowed down by decorative UI. It should feel like a serious options terminal: precise, compact, technical, and quietly premium.
keyCharacteristics: Black terminal surface with thin white-alpha strokes and minimal tonal layering.
High information density with local controls placed beside the chart they affect.
Highcharts SVG chart language: thin rounded lines, small circular points, dark SVG tooltips, and restrained data colors.
One technical sans family, Outfit, used across labels, titles, numbers, controls, and chart text.
Honest state language: live, stale, reconnecting, unavailable, and insufficient-history states are part of the design system.

rules.name: The Data-Role Rule
rules.body: Every saturated color must explain a data category, a state, or a selected control. If the color is only making the page feel more exciting, remove it.

rules.name: The Black Surface Rule
rules.body: Panels, toolbars, charts, and tooltips stay on Terminal Black. Depth comes from border strength, text hierarchy, and chart geometry, not from stacked gray cards.

rules.name: The No Display Drama Rule
rules.body: This is a product surface. Do not introduce oversized marketing headings, fluid hero type, or display-font labels.

rules.name: The Numeric Clarity Rule
rules.body: Numbers must be formatted for scanning: compact units for large values, percentages with controlled decimals, and dates as yyyy/mm/dd when shown to users.

rules.name: The Flat Terminal Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. If a panel needs emphasis, strengthen the border or improve hierarchy; do not add a soft card shadow.

dos: Do keep the surface black, dense, and terminal-like. This product is for traders reading live market structure.
Do use saturated colors only for chart roles, semantic state, or selected controls.
Do keep chart controls local to the chart panel they affect.
Do label units directly in KPI titles, table headers, or tooltips.
Do show stale, reconnecting, unavailable, and insufficient-history states honestly.
Do preserve Highcharts-style SVG tooltips, thin rounded line strokes, small circle markers, and restrained axis labels.
Do test text and chart labels on mobile so controls, annotations, and titles do not overlap.
donts: Don't use generic SaaS dashboard card grids.
Don't use marketing-page composition, oversized hero sections, or homepage-style value propositions inside the dashboard.
Don't reduce information density to make the surface feel friendlier; improve structure instead.
Don't add ornamental gradients, glass effects, decorative shadows, or arbitrary colorful accents.
Don't make unavailable or statistically weak metrics look complete.
Don't rely on red/green alone to communicate call/put or positive/negative meaning; pair it with labels, legends, signs, or position.
Don't add soft card shadows to bordered panels. That breaks the terminal material.
Don't introduce display fonts, novelty mono labels, or fluid hero typography into operational UI.
